description: Test your knowledge of epithelial types, their locations, and functions.

cards.text: Which epithelial type lines the alveoli and enables gas diffusion?

tip.tip: Think about the thinnest possible barrier for gas exchange

cards.text: What type of epithelium lines the trachea and bronchi?

tip.tip: All cells touch the basement membrane but nuclei are at different levels

cards.text: Which epithelium lines the urinary bladder and can stretch?

tip.tip: Dome-shaped cells when relaxed, flat when distended

cards.answer: Replacement of one epithelial type by stratified squamous epithelium due to chronic irritation

tip.tip: Common in smokers' airways - protective but pre-malignant

cards.text: Which epithelium lines the small intestine for absorption?

tip.tip: Tall cells with oval basal nuclei and goblet cells

cards.text: What are the three components of the junctional complex from apical to basal?
cards.answer: Zonula occludens, zonula adherens, macula adherens (desmosome)

cards.text: Where is keratinised stratified squamous epithelium found?

tip.tip: Dry surface exposed to air with anucleate keratin-filled surface cells

cards.text: Which epithelial type lines kidney tubules and thyroid follicles?

tip.tip: Cube-shaped cells with round central nuclei, height equals width

cards.text: What is the basement membrane composed of?
cards.answer: Basal lamina (lamina lucida and lamina densa) and reticular lamina

tip.tip: Separates epithelium from underlying connective tissue

cards.text: Name the site where squamous-columnar junction is clinically important for cervical cancer screening.
cards.answer: Transformation zone of the uterine cervix

tip.tip: Pap smear samples cells from this junction
